introSection: Takabisha (Japanese: 高飛車) is a steel launched diving roller coaster located at Fuji-Q Highland in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi, Japan. Upon opening in July 2011, the ride featured the world's steepest drop at 121°, half a degree more than Green Lantern Coaster at Warner Bros. Movie World. In 2019, this record was succeeded by TMNT Shellraiser, a near clone of Takabisha at Nickelodeon Universe in New Jersey, which has a drop that is half a degree steeper than Takabisha's.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Takabisha begins with an enclosed section that includes a heartline roll and the launch track. The second half of the ride begins with a vertical chain lift hill and a beyond vertical drop.

fullText:
Takabisha (Japanese: 高飛車) is a steel launched diving roller coaster located at Fuji-Q Highland in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi, Japan. Upon opening in July 2011, the ride featured the world's steepest drop at 121°, half a degree more than Green Lantern Coaster at Warner Bros. Movie World. In 2019, this record was succeeded by TMNT Shellraiser, a near clone of Takabisha at Nickelodeon Universe in New Jersey, which has a drop that is half a degree steeper than Takabisha's.

Takabisha begins with an enclosed section that includes a heartline roll and the launch track. The second half of the ride begins with a vertical chain lift hill and a beyond vertical drop.
